summary: The old plural map blinking loop is commented out and its referenced client files are absent; the maintained path is the singular per-unit Client_BlinkMapIcon compile.
evidence: Commented references point at missing Client/Functions/Client_BlinkMapIcons.sqf and Client/Functions/Client_AddUnitToTrack.sqf.
The live compile uses Client/Functions/Client_BlinkMapIcon.sqf.
riskIfRemoved: Low if only removing comments/docs residue. Do not remove the singular live blink helper.
suggestedAction: Clean stale comments or leave as historical note; keep current per-unit blink implementation documented.
validation: Source search for plural helper references after cleanup.
Map markers still blink through Client_BlinkMapIcon where intended.

summary: The old server-side map blinking executor is commented and points to a missing Server_MapBlinkingUnits.sqf file.
evidence: Commented execVM references Server/Functions/Server_MapBlinkingUnits.sqf, which is not present in the maintained mission roots.
riskIfRemoved: Low for comment cleanup. High only if someone tries to revive it without resolving client marker ownership.
suggestedAction: Treat as retired; do not revive unless a marker authority design is written first.
validation: Repo search for Server_MapBlinkingUnits after cleanup.
Marker behavior smoke if any marker code is touched.

summary: Legacy RscMenu_Upgrade still exists and its onLoad points at missing Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf, while current upgrade flow uses WFBE_UpgradeMenu / GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf.
evidence: Dialog class remains in Rsc/Dialogs.hpp.
onLoad references Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf, absent from the source mission.
Existing docs and source searches route current upgrade behavior through GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Safe only if no script opens RscMenu_Upgrade dynamically; otherwise a menu path will fail at runtime.
suggestedAction: Search all open-dialog/createDialog paths, then remove or alias the stale class to the maintained upgrade menu.
validation: Static search for RscMenu_Upgrade callers.
Open upgrade menu in hosted and dedicated sessions.

summary: MASH tents remain real, but the public-variable marker relay is only half wired: server listener compiles, client receiver compile is commented, and modded missions contain sender drift.
evidence: Server compiles WFBE_SE_FNC_MASH_MARKER.
Client receiver compile is commented.
Server listens for WFBE_CL_MASH_MARKER_CREATED; client receiver listens for WFBE_SE_MASH_MARKER_SENT but is not registered in source Chernarus.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing only marker relay should not remove MASH spawn/respawn functionality, but it can erase useful revive work.
suggestedAction: Either retire the marker relay explicitly or revive it with a single owner page, PV channel entry, sender/receiver registration and marker lifecycle smoke.
validation: Hosted/dedicated test for MASH creation, marker creation, JIP marker state and deletion.
PV channel audit for WFBE_CL_MASH_MARKER_CREATED and WFBE_SE_MASH_MARKER_SENT.

summary: AIBuyUnit compiles Server_BuyUnit.sqf, but stable source search finds no live caller; it is an AI/server production worker, not current player-buy parity.
evidence: Repo-wide source search finds the compile and worker body but no stable caller outside documentation.
Worker aborts when the factory dies or a player replaces the AI leader, matching AI/team production semantics.
riskIfRemoved: High. Future AI commander branch work may intentionally call it, and it duplicates vehicle setup logic that documents server-side production assumptions.
suggestedAction: Do not delete casually. Mark latent until AI commander production is merged or formally retired; if revived, compare against Client_BuildUnit.sqf drift.
validation: Source search for AIBuyUnit on target branch.
AI commander production smoke if branch code wires it.

summary: The maintained modded mission directories still contain real merge conflict markers across config, client, skill, RHUD, nuke and root files.
evidence: The scan found 18 text files containing ^<<<<<<<, ^======= or ^>>>>>>> conflict-marker lines under Modded_Missions.
Tools/LoadoutManager/ZipManager.cs currently packages Missions and Missions_Vanilla only, leaving Modded_Missions outside the normal release path.
riskIfRemoved: Medium to high. Markers must be resolved by choosing the correct source branch content, not blindly deleting sections.
suggestedAction: Treat modded missions as not release-ready until conflicts are resolved and LoadoutManager modded packaging policy is decided.
validation: Run conflict-marker scan after resolution.
Run modded mission copy/pack workflow only when modded maps are intentionally re-enabled.

summary: description.ext contains a commented include for scripts/unitCaching/description.ext, but that scaffold is absent from the repo.
evidence: The include is commented.
No scripts/unitCaching/description.ext exists in the maintained source mission.
riskIfRemoved: Low. It is disabled and not a boot dependency.
suggestedAction: Remove the commented include or keep it only as a documented abandoned optimization lead.
validation: Mission config preprocess still loads after comment cleanup.

summary: Init_Common.sqf still contains a commented compile for Common_HandleBombs.sqf, which is absent; current bomb/missile handling lives elsewhere.
evidence: Commented HandleBombs compile points at missing Common/Functions/Common_HandleBombs.sqf.
riskIfRemoved: Low if only removing the commented compile.
suggestedAction: Clean the comment or document as retired pre-IRS/bomb handling scaffold.
validation: Search for HandleBombs after cleanup.
Smoke IRS/missile warning systems only if live handlers are edited.

summary: Faction defense files still carry commented calls to missing Common/Config/Config_Defenses.sqf; live town defense registration uses Config_Defenses_Towns.sqf.
evidence: Live code calls Config_Defenses_Towns.sqf when isServer.
The older Config_Defenses.sqf call is commented and the target file is absent.
riskIfRemoved: Low for comments; medium if someone expects base defenses to be revived from the old helper name.
suggestedAction: Remove/comment-rationalize the old Config_Defenses.sqf references after confirming no branch needs them.
validation: Town defense categories still register on server.
Search for active Config_Defenses.sqf references after cleanup.

summary: WASP client init contains commented hooks for missing KeyDown, OnArmor timer and SitsOnArmor scripts; some adjacent WASP action code remains live or partially live, so cleanup must be narrow.
evidence: Missing commented references include WASP/actions/OnArmor/timer.sqf, WASP/KeyDown.sqf and WASP/actions/SitsOnArmor/init.sqf.
WASP_procInitComm is compiled in initJIPCompatible.sqf and is used by wheel repair action code, so the whole WASP folder is not dead.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing commented missing hooks is safe; deleting adjacent WASP action helpers may break player actions or marker behavior.
suggestedAction: Only clean commented missing hooks unless a source-backed WASP action inventory proves more code is unreachable.
validation: WASP action smoke for repair/wheel behavior if action files are edited.
Search for KeyDown/OnArmor/SitsOnArmor after cleanup.

summary: WFBE_C_MODULE_BIS_HC is exposed as a parameter, but no static consumer was found; headless delegation uses WFBE_C_AI_DELEGATION instead.
evidence: Parameter row exists.
Current headless-client selection/delegation paths use WFBE_C_AI_DELEGATION, not WFBE_C_MODULE_BIS_HC.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. It may be intended for a future BIS High Command UI feature, but current users can mistake it for HC enablement.
suggestedAction: Hide/remove the parameter or wire a real Arma 2 OA High Command feature; update operator docs either way.
validation: Lobby parameter list matches runtime behavior.
Dedicated HC delegation still follows WFBE_C_AI_DELEGATION.

summary: GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf writes controls 23004, 23005 and 23006, but the visible RscMenu_Economy declaration in the source snippet starts at 23002/23003 then 23008+, suggesting stale or missing controls.
evidence: GUI_Menu_Economy uses ctrlEnable/ctrlSetText for 23004/23005/23006.
Audited RscMenu_Economy declaration did not show those IDC values in the nearby control set.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. A runtime control lookup may silently fail or produce UI errors depending on Arma 2 behavior and actual include expansion.
suggestedAction: Complete a full RscMenu_Economy IDC inventory, then either restore intended controls or remove stale writes.
validation: Open economy menu as commander with HQ deployed and undeployed.
Search all Rsc files for idc 23004/23005/23006 before patching.

summary: Some modded mission init files compile Common_GetTotalCamps helpers that the source Chernarus/Takistan trees contain, but modded roots appear inconsistent and need direct per-map file restoration before re-enabling modded packaging.
evidence: Reference scan reports missing Common_GetTotalCamps.sqf and Common_GetTotalCampsOnSide.sqf in multiple modded roots.
Maintained source roots have equivalent helpers; modded roots require map-by-map confirmation before packaging.
riskIfRemoved: High if modded missions are revived. Missing helper compiles can break town/camp logic early in init.
suggestedAction: Restore helpers from the correct maintained source root or regenerate modded missions from current source before releasing modded maps.
validation: Per-map file existence check.
Launch modded mission init through editor/dedicated smoke after conflict markers are resolved.

summary: version.sqf is included by mission entrypoints but is generated/ignored by LoadoutManager, so clean checkouts rely on the generation/copy workflow before packaging.
evidence: Mission source includes version.sqf.
LoadoutManager generates version.sqf and file management excludes generated version artifacts from normal source treatment.
riskIfRemoved: High if misunderstood as dead code. The include is live, but the file is generated.
suggestedAction: Keep generated-file contract documented; do not remove includes unless replacing the version system.
validation: Clean checkout build/copy workflow produces version.sqf before mission load.
Docs explain missing version.sqf as workflow issue, not dead code.

summary: RscClickableText has a malformed soundPush array with an empty first element token, which looks like stale resource config rather than dead code.
evidence: soundPush[] = {, 0.2, 1}; appears in maintained source and modded copies.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. It may cause config parse warnings or broken click sounds; changing resource bases can affect many dialogs.
suggestedAction: Replace with an explicit empty string or a valid sound path after config smoke, then propagate through generated/maintained roots.
validation: Mission config loads with no resource parse error.
Open representative dialogs using RscClickableText.

summary: LoadoutManager packaging intentionally skips Modded_Missions while a TODO says to add modded maps back later; this explains why broken modded sources have not affected current release artifacts.
evidence: ZipManager missionDirectories contains Missions and Missions_Vanilla, with Modded_Missions commented out.
SqfFileGenerator carries a TODO to add modded maps back later.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Re-enabling packaging before resolving conflicts and missing helpers will ship broken missions.
suggestedAction: Keep modded packaging disabled until the modded source repair checklist passes, or archive the old modded trees as historical imports.
validation: Conflict-marker scan clean.
Missing-reference scan clean for targeted modded roots.
LoadoutManager pack smoke after re-enabling.

summary: DiscordBot has a FileConfiguration.DataSourcePath/botconfig.json helper, but the live status-data reader bypasses it and reads Preferences.Instance.DataSourcePath directly.
evidence: FileConfiguration.DataSourcePath can read botconfig.json and fall back to C:\a2waspwarfare\Data.
GameData.LoadFromFile resolves dataPath from Preferences.Instance.DataSourcePath or the hardcoded default instead of FileConfiguration.DataSourcePath.
Static search found FileConfiguration.LogsPath used by logging, while DataSourcePath is not used by the live game-status JSON reader.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing the whole class would break logging; removing only the dormant data-path branch requires deciding whether botconfig.json is intentionally unsupported.
suggestedAction: Choose one DiscordBot configuration source. Either wire GameData.LoadFromFile through FileConfiguration.DataSourcePath or delete/demote the botconfig.json data-path helper while keeping LogsPath or replacing it.
validation: DiscordBot starts with valid preferences.json and token.txt.
Status updater reads database.json from the documented source path.
Logging still writes to the intended .logs directory.

summary: GameDataDeSerialization.HandleGameDataCreationOrLoading is still callable and uses TypeNameHandling.Auto, but the startup/timer/command status paths now load through GameData.LoadFromFile instead.
evidence: ProgramRuntime and Ready handler call GameData.LoadFromFile via DeSerializeGameDataFromExtension.
Timer and command paths also call GameData.LoadFromFile.
HandleGameDataCreationOrLoading remains public and deserializes caller-provided JSON with TypeNameHandling.Auto.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. It appears outside the live status path, but deleting it without a build/test can break any future command or test harness that still expects it.
suggestedAction: Delete it, make it private, or force TypeNameHandling.None before any future caller can revive it.
validation: DiscordBot build succeeds.
Normal database.json fixture still loads.
Hostile JSON with $type metadata is ignored or rejected if this helper is kept.

summary: The in-repo a2waspwarfare_Extension active writer is one-way, but SerializationManager still contains a commented persistence load scaffold using TypeNameHandling.Auto and old Database/League references.
evidence: SerializeDB is active and uses TypeNameHandling.None.
The DeSerializeDB / HandleDatabaseCreationOrLoading block is commented out.
The commented block references TypeNameHandling.Auto and old Database persistence types, while the SQF caller only sends GLOBALGAMESTATS and discards output.
riskIfRemoved: Low for current runtime if only deleting commented code; high if revived naively because it reintroduces unsafe type-name deserialization and stale persistence types.
suggestedAction: Remove the commented scaffold or replace it with a safe DTO-only loader before any extension persistence revival.
validation: Extension project still builds in the intended .NET Framework 4.8 x86 toolchain.
GlobalGameStats.sqf still writes database.json through the active path.

summary: GLOBALGAMESTATS now sends five data fields after the class selector, but code comments/default arrays still describe older smaller shapes in both the extension writer and DiscordBot reader.
evidence: GlobalGameStats.sqf sends class name plus west score, east score, map, uptime and player count.
GLOBALGAMESTATS comments still mark uptime/player count as TODO and Extension GameData initializes exportedArgs to length 2.
DiscordBot initializes exportedArgs to length 4 while player-count display checks index 4 only when Length > 4.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. The current serialized file can carry the longer array, but fallback/default GameData cannot represent all fields, and stale comments mislead future integration work.
suggestedAction: Define the database.json contract once, align default array sizes/comments, and add fixture tests for short, normal and long arrays.
validation: Extension fixture writes five expected fields.
DiscordBot displays map, score, uptime and player count from a normal database.json.
Short/missing arrays fall back intentionally without misleading status.

summary: DiscordBot carries terrain classes under SharedWithLoadoutManager, including a WriteToFile mission-generation API, but the bot only uses terrain metadata for display and no caller invokes the write API.
evidence: BaseTerrain.WriteToFile constructs mission paths and writes files, copied from generator-style terrain code.
Static search found no DiscordBot caller for WriteToFile; GameData only resolves a terrain instance for display/player-count metadata.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing the method requires changing InterfaceTerrain and all terrain implementations compile shape, but runtime status display should not need mission file writing.
suggestedAction: Split DiscordBot display metadata from LoadoutManager generation APIs, or explicitly document this folder as copied/shared historical code until refactored.
validation: DiscordBot build succeeds after removing/demoting WriteToFile.
Status embed/channel name still resolve Chernarus/Takistan terrain display and player caps.

summary: DiscordBot still includes TASMANIA2010 terrain metadata even though LoadoutManager's current terrain enum no longer includes Tasmania and upstream history documents Tasmania as removed after map/version failures.
evidence: DiscordBot terrain enum/class include TASMANIA2010.
Tools/LoadoutManager TerrainName enum does not list TASMANIA2010 in the current source.
Mission boundary code still has a generic tasmania2010 case, but there is no maintained Tasmania mission folder in the current source roots.
riskIfRemoved: Low to medium. If a production database.json can still emit TASMANIA2010, DiscordBot display would lose a fallback; otherwise this is stale metadata.
suggestedAction: Confirm whether production can still emit TASMANIA2010. If not, remove or archive the DiscordBot Tasmania class; if yes, document it as legacy display-only metadata.
validation: DiscordBot handles unknown/removed map names with a safe fallback.
No maintained release path claims Tasmania support without a regenerated mission and clean-load smoke.

summary: The in-repo BattlEye footprint is only the AFK kick publicVariable rule and its README, so it must not be described as broad public-server hardening.
evidence: Tracked BattlEyeFilter files are the README docx and publicvariable.txt.
publicvariable.txt contains only //new and 5 "kickAFK".
Client update FSM broadcasts kickAFK for that customized filter; no in-repo scripts.txt/server.cfg/basic.cfg or broader command filters were found.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing the AFK filter breaks the existing kick trick; overclaiming it hides major authority gaps.
suggestedAction: Keep as AFK feature plumbing. Require owner-provided production BEpath evidence before claiming public-server hardening; design broader OA-era filters separately if desired.
validation: AFK kick still works on servers that install the filter.
Public-server release notes do not claim broad BattlEye protection from this repo alone.

summary: LoadoutManager has warning-named CRV7PG weapon/ammunition classes marked DO_NOT_USE_IN_LOADOUTS, yet WILDCAT references the warning ammunition, so the warning is active data debt rather than dead code.
evidence: Weapon/ammunition types and class names explicitly warn that CRV7PG can crash the game if used in loadouts.
WILDCAT.cs currently references WARNING_GAME_CRASH_DO_NOT_USE_IN_LOADOUTS_TWELVEROUNDCRV7PG in its loadout data.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing the classes can break the generator; leaving the reference can preserve a known crash-risk loadout.
suggestedAction: Do not treat as dead. Either remove the WILDCAT reference and regenerate/smoke, or rename/encode the warning as a validation failure in the generator.
validation: LoadoutManager build/generation succeeds.
Arma 2 OA aircraft smoke confirms WILDCAT loadout does not crash when selected/equipped.

summary: Six old WFBE_* direct publicVariable channel names now appear only in comments beside live PVF/SendToServer/SendToClients paths, making them migration residue rather than active networking contracts.
evidence: The direct PV scan reports WFBE_ChangeScore, WFBE_LocalizeMessage, WFBE_RequestSpecial, WFBE_RequestStructure, WFBE_RequestTeamUpdate and WFBE_RequestVehicleLock as comment-only channels.

The same files show live or intended calls through HandleSPVF, WFBE_CO_FNC_SendToServer, WFBE_CO_FNC_SendToClients or PVF registrations such as RequestVehicleLock, RequestSpecial, RequestTeamUpdate, RequestStructure, RequestChangeScore and LocalizeMessage.
riskIfRemoved: Low if only removing or rewriting comments. High if a future branch still documents those exact direct WFBE_* variables as active network contracts.
suggestedAction: Clean or annotate the comments as legacy direct-PV names after branch-aware search; keep the PVF registrations and helper calls untouched.
validation: Rerun dead-code-pv-channel-scan.ps1 and confirm the six comment-only channels disappear or are intentionally documented.
Open MHQ lock, special/economy, construction, commander team update and score-change flows if adjacent live code is edited.

summary: Dedicated server FPS is published through two direct PV names; source/Vanilla RHUD consumes SERVER_FPS_GUI, while WFBE_VAR_SERVER_FPS has no current source/Vanilla UI reader and only stale/modded consumer evidence.
evidence: serverFpsGUI.sqf publishes SERVER_FPS_GUI.
monitorServerFPS.sqf publishes WFBE_VAR_SERVER_FPS.
Source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla RHUD read SERVER_FPS_GUI.
Lingor modded RHUD still has a WFBE_VAR_SERVER_FPS wait/read path, so consolidation is compatibility cleanup rather than a safe deletion.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. Removing WFBE_VAR_SERVER_FPS can break stale/modded RHUD consumers; keeping both preserves redundant publisher work and confusing docs.
suggestedAction: Choose one FPS contract deliberately. Prefer SERVER_FPS_GUI for source/Vanilla UI, but keep or shim WFBE_VAR_SERVER_FPS until modded/stale consumers are either migrated or archived.
validation: Dedicated server publishes whichever FPS contract remains about every 8 seconds.
RHUD FPS renders in source Chernarus, maintained Vanilla and any revived modded mission.
Hosted/listen server still avoids the old busy-loop shape.

summary: Client update FSM registers an ICBM_launched publicVariable event handler, but the maintained source/Vanilla nuke path does not set or publicVariable that channel; current display/damage flow uses NukeIncoming plus RequestSpecial and HandleSpecial icbm-display.
evidence: The direct PV scan sees ICBM_launched receivers in source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla but no sender.
GUI_Menu_Tactical spawns NukeIncoming directly; nukeincoming sends RequestSpecial ICBM to the server and broadcasts HandleSpecial icbm-display to clients.
Client HandleSpecial dispatches icbm-display to WFBE_CL_FNC_Display_ICBM; Server_HandleSpecial applies NukeDammage after the target dies.
riskIfRemoved: Medium. The stale handler may be unused today, but ICBM is a live tactical feature and old branches may still depend on this notification model.
suggestedAction: Do not delete the nuke feature. Either remove the ICBM_launched PVEH/commented receiver pair as legacy wiring or revive it intentionally with a real sender and single documented nuke notification path.
validation: Tactical ICBM smoke: commander purchase, friendly/enemy warning, marker lifecycle, server-side damage and client display.
Rerun direct PV scan to confirm no accidental receiver-only channel remains after cleanup.
